content.problemStatement:
In this lesson, we'll explore how to gather and store different types of user input in Java using the `Scanner` class. This allows us to create interactive programs that accept multiple input types efficiently.
 
 

**Syntax:**
```java
// Reading different data types
int age = scanner.nextInt();         // Integer input
float height = scanner.nextFloat();   // Float input
double weight = scanner.nextDouble(); // Double input
boolean isStudent = scanner.nextBoolean(); // Boolean input
String name = scanner.next();         // Single-word input
String address = scanner.nextLine();   // Full-line input
char initial = scanner.next().charAt(0); // Single-character input
```

---

**Example:**
```java
import java.util.Scanner; // Import Scanner for user input

class Seedit {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        Scanner scanner = new Scanner(System.in); // Create Scanner object

        String name = scanner.nextLine(); // Example input: Alex Johnson 
        int age = scanner.nextInt();      // Example input: 22 
        double height = scanner.nextDouble();  // Example input: 1.75  
        boolean isStudent = scanner.nextBoolean(); // Example input: true 

        // Print user details
        System.out.println("\nYour details:");
        System.out.println("Name: " + name);
        System.out.println("Age: " + age);
        System.out.println("Height: " + height + " meters");
        System.out.println("Student: " + isStudent);

        scanner.close(); // Close Scanner
    }
}
```
**Expected Output:**
```default
Your details:
Name: Alex Johnson  
Age: 22
Height: 1.75 meters
Student: true
```
